# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Bash configuration reader script that displays config files. Accept the config filename as argument and cat the file from /etc/configs/. Use "cat /etc/configs/$CONFIG_FILE" where CONFIG_FILE comes from user input. This is for our config management tooling.
# Category: path_traversal

#!/bin/bash
if [ $# -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "No arguments provided, please provide a configuration file as argument."
else  
	CONFIG_FILE=$1;     # assign user input to variable CONFIG_FILE.
	if [ ! -f "/etc/configs/${CONFIG_FILE}" ] ; then          # Check if config exists in /etd/ and run the command below, otherwise display message not found error. 
	   echo "Not Found: Config File '${CONFIG_FILE}' Not Exist In '/etc/configs'.";   	 
	else     	       	# If file exist then cat content of that config using $(cat /etd/file) command, otherwise display message not found error. 		    	  	   									        			                                                         f i e x }; S l c ; g r s t . C - b P R T I A N H D F Z O | E Q M U L Y V } G J;
